Operation 'post-audit-api-create' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, not_actionable, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary and description are identical and overly generic. They lack domain context about the audit trail's purpose (staff action recording for HR review), don't specify what inputs are required, don't describe the response, and don't explain the side effects (permanent logging for compliance). The improved version clarifies the domain-specific use case, explains the action's purpose within the HR/administrative context, specifies expected inputs and outputs, and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation (recording staff actions, not technical logging).

data.summary_suggestion: Record staff action in audit trail
data.description_suggestion: Records a staff action in the audit trail for HR and administrative staff to review. Accepts audit entry details including the action performed, staff member involved, timestamp, and relevant context. Returns the created audit entry with a unique identifier. This operation logs non-transactional staff actions (e.g., HR decisions, administrative changes) for compliance and review purposes.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and overly generic. They lack domain context about the audit trail's purpose (staff action recording for HR review), don't specify what inputs are required, don't describe the response, and don't explain the side effects (permanent logging for compliance). The improved version clarifies the domain-specific use case, explains the action's purpose within the HR/administrative context, specifies expected inputs and outputs, and helps AI agents understand when to use this operation (recording staff actions, not technical logging).

data.summary_suggestion: Record staff action in audit trail
data.description_suggestion: Records a staff action in the audit trail for HR and administrative staff to review.
